ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Weinberg, via Zoom, specially appearing for and with LaTisa Brooks, Petitioner; Jerry Hauser, via Zoom, for and with Helen Redwood, Objector.

The court points to Ms. Brooks' objection pending in the court's e-fling queue and reminds Mr. Hauser that accountings are typically filed in two-year increments. Mr. Hauser reports as to the filing of the accounting and offers additional testimony and requests that the court consider taking the petition set for hearing today off calendar. Mr. Weinberg agrees that advises that he is cautiously optimistic that the parties can resolve this matter and agrees that his client's petition can be taken off calendar. The court reminds Mr. Weinberg to file a substitution of attorney.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Continued To Jul-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-13-2022 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Latisa Brooks, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner; Jerry Hauser, via ZOOM, for Helen Redwood, via ZOOM, Objector.

The court questions Mr. Hauser as to why there has not been an accounting. Mr. Hauser reports that the main asset in the trust is real property wherein the decedent's wish was for the home to remain in the family and that via the agreement by the siblings, the simplest thing to do was to appoint one person to be the executor. Mr. Hauser reports that the real property does not generate any income but that the home had been refinanced to buy-out one of the siblings and to make repairs to the property and that the petitioner had been provided with information regarding that transaction. Mr. Hauser advises that there is an issue regarding Ms. Brooks' request for an accounting going back to 2004 and believes that the accounting should date back from when Ms. Brooks became a beneficiary in 2013. Mr. Hauser provides additional testimony and states that the intent of the family was to follow the intentions of the grandparents. The court advises Ms. Brooks that the accounting from when she and her sister became beneficiaries can be made available to her directly without the court's review or, if she wishes the court to review the accountings, they would require to be submitted to the court in two-year increments. Ms. Brooks advises that she would like the accountings submitted directly to her with no court involvement. Mr. Hauser reports that he is ready to provide Ms. Brooks with the accountings for eight years.

The Petition to Compel Trustee Accounting is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Continued To Jun-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Latisa Brooks (Petitioner) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Jerry Hauser appeared by zoom for and with Helen Redwood (Trustee) by zoom.

The Court stated that it received a proposed order, that it will go through it and revise it as necessary, and that it seems this case would be perfect for pro bono mediation. Mr. Hauser stated that he has not seen the proposed order, that the tentative ruling did not state any findings, that he does not know what is in the order, so he does not know whether he is objecting to the order and that he has no objection to participating in mediation. Ms. Brooks stated that she will e-mail the proposed order to Mr. Hauser. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than June 15, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by June 22, 2021. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account is continued to and the Parties are to return on June 29, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the mediation order.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Continued To Apr-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Latisa Brooks appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Jerry Hauser appeared by zoom for and with Helen Redwood (Trustee) by zoom.
Mandisa Brooks (Beneficiary) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Tinisha Bishop McConner (Beneficiary) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that there are notice deficiencies, stated that it sent out a notice to Latisa Brooks and it has not received anything back, that notice needs to be provided to everyone and that a proof of service is needed. Latisa stated that she submitted a proof of service on December 22, 2021, that she put in the box in the lobby at the direction from the Bailiff, that she has been in touch with everyone and that the proof of service is in the possession of the Court. The Court stated that it did not get the proof of service, that Latisa should have followed up on it, that she needs to file a new proof of service, that everyone needs to be familiar with the ninth amended general order and informed Latisa that the Clerk's office is now open in the morning. Latisa is to file the proof of service with the original hearing date and the original date of mailing. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account is continued to April 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
